    <item>
      <title>Bulls face major hurdle to find a new GM</title>
      <description>After years of being stuck in the middle, not making the most of their assets, and failing to contend, the Chicago Bulls finally fired GM Marc Eversley and executive vice president of basketball operations Arturas Karnisovas.</description>
      <pubDate>08 Apr 2026 17:08:12 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/bulls_face_major_hurdle_to_find_a_new_gm/s1_13132_43703064</link>

        <media:credit> Kamil Krzaczynski-Imagn Images </media:credit>
        <media:title> Arturas Karnisovas (right) talks with former Bulls general manager Marc Eversley (left) before game three of the first round of the 2022 NBA playoffs against the Milwaukee Bucks at United Center. </media:title>
      </media:content>
      <category>NBA</category>
      <category>Chicago Bulls</category>
    </item>
    <item>
      <title>NBA investigating Kings for bizarre play vs. Warriors in wake of Draymond Green complaints</title>
      <description>The NBA has only disciplined two teams for trying to lose this season. Now they're investigating the Sacramento Kings for a simple reason: The league got embarrassed.</description>

    <item>
      <title>Pistons' Cade Cunningham has triumphant return vs. Bucks</title>

    <item>
      <title>Heat stumble into play-in for fourth straight season</title>
